author: @Nico-vh1qp
text: this would be great for preventing the red ring of death on the Xbox and easier than the penny trick.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: The red ring of death, I have not heard of that in a while lol, I used to put a damp towel over the Xbox then put it in the freezer LOL. That did the trick for a day or 2 hahaha.

text:
Thank you,for the update! 
 I am a becoming a bit suspicious, 
that you are just having fun,with the computer games,  
~much like Rob, who is also sloping off, 
possibly on a pub crawl,,,

and showing us the beautiful sights! Lol!😁
I hope you  all have a great weekend!!😊
Andrea🙋‍♀️, Jasper🐕‍🦺 and George the Pigeon.🕊️ XxX.

author: @BoB4jjjjs
text: You just wanted to play games instead of working!  :-D  Let's see what it is like in another six months!  Or maybe a year!  EDIT:  Will it dry out.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Hahahaha thats maybe so, don't tell Rob LOL. I have not taken the cooler off since I have put the ink in, I think it has dried out, but I think this has also improved the thermal conductivity? I can't wait to take a look soon! Thanks mate! :)

author: @synchro-dentally1965
text: Thanks for the update Luke. I really think that if you put the ink in a vacuum chamber and maximize the removal of any air bubbles before applying it to the heat sink and cpu you'll get a better heat transfer performance.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I really like this suggestion! When I replace the ink in the computer I will do this! Thank you mate! :)

author: @stubluesjhb1
text: Did I miss you saying what the ambient temps for the two tests were, because it will have a bearing on temps

author: @shay4578
text:
Cheers.
Just wanted to say that, as a parent to a 6yo child, I developed an aversion to hearing cheerful young people loudly welcoming me with a smile.

I know it sounds weird but it's true.

Robert's introductions are much more pleasant to my ears because of that.

It may just be me asking but if you could tone down the intro to the level you used during the explanation of the ink's performance during testing that would be an improvement I'd like to hear.

Sorry for the rant.

author: @darina3542
text: <thermal conductivity of 12.5W/mK, making it the most thermally conductive ceramic paste> this one is best?  how much did you measure the ink at?

author: @DiyEcoProjects
text: Hi Luke, wow has it realy been 6 months already? Congrats and well done on your experiments. All the best, kieron

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: Time really flies when you're having fun lol!  Thank you mate! :)

author: @ScarletFlames1
text:
Doesn't it do thermal throttling at 80c? You'd need a program that can follow the dynamic changes in CPU cycles to see how much it throttles if your tests reach thermal throttling limits. Or do the unsafe thing and disable thermal throttling (don't, unless you're prepared to buy a new CPU if\when that one fries itself).

3DMark did have a graph for that.. I do believe it should have been shown in the video.

author: @l0I0I0I0
text: Was wondering, can the ink be used as an adhesive and does it dry hard/ solid like regular ink?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I hope so! This has really caught my interest, I think it definitely has potential. Thank you mate! :)
